* Fix merge ralph: prevent duplicate conflict issues and unblock draft PR advancement (#582)

* Initial plan

* Fix merge ralph: guard against duplicate work and fix draft PR advancement

Two bugs fixed:

1. Added guard in run_merge to check for existing pending issues or tracked
   PRs before creating new conflict resolution work. This prevents duplicate
   issues from being filed for the same merge conflict.

2. Updated the Draft PR handler in advance_tracked_prs to also check
   is_pr_work_completed() when the agent is still assigned to the issue.
   Some agents (e.g. Copilot) remain assigned after finishing, so the
   two-tier check (session completion + title heuristic) is now consulted
   before deciding to skip. This mirrors the same pattern already used in
   check_agent_status.

Also made is_pr_work_completed public so it can be called from headless.rs.

Fix unstuck PR loop to advance draft PRs instead of incorrectly skipping them (#609)

* Initial plan

* Fix unstuck PR loop to use check_pr_merge_status like advance_issue_pr

The main PR loop in run_unstuck only called has_failing_checks_for_pr(),
which returns false for draft PRs with no checks, incorrectly logging
"checks OK, skipping". Now uses check_pr_merge_status() to properly
handle draft PRs (marking them ready for review), agent WIP, not-yet-
mergeable PRs (checking failures, approving workflows, enabling auto-
merge), and mergeable PRs — matching the advancement logic already used
in advance_issue_pr.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

[CI] Add manifest diff workflow (Part 2/3) (#3301)

## Motivation

Add a reusable GitHub Actions workflow for generating manifest diff
reports on-demand. This workflow enables both manual triggering and
integration with other CI workflows.

[**Example
Report**](https://therock-ci-artifacts.s3.amazonaws.com/23025588012-linux/logs/manifest-diff/TheRockReport.html)

This is Part 2/3 of the manifest diff report feature (split from #2161).
- Part 1: #3295 - Python scripts and tests
- Part 2: This PR - Reusable workflow
- Part 3: CI nightly integration (depends on this PR)

## Technical details

- **`.github/workflows/manifest-diff.yml`**
  - `workflow_dispatch` for manual runs (GitHub UI or CLI).
  - `workflow_call` for use by other workflows (e.g. `ci_nightly.yml`).
- Single “Generate manifest diff report” step: all inputs are passed
through to the script; the script validates combinations and reports
clear errors for invalid input.
- Supports the same four input combinations (see below); validation and
behavior live in the script, not in workflow conditionals.
- S3 upload of the generated HTML report via
`upload_test_report_script.py` (when not from a fork).

- **`build_tools/github_actions/upload_test_report_script.py`**
- Appends the S3 report URL to the job step summary via
`gha_append_step_summary` so the report link appears in the Actions UI.
Safe when not in GHA (no-op if `GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY` is unset).

- **Script and template (supporting changes)**  
- Script: optional args from the workflow (e.g. empty `--start` /
`--find-last-successful`) are normalized and validated so invalid
combinations fail with a clear message.
- Template: commit range arrow and section icons use HTML entities and
`<meta charset="utf-8">` so the report displays correctly and avoids
encoding issues (e.g. mojibake in the commit range line).


Input combinations supported:
- `find_last_successful` + `workflow_mode`: Find start from last
successful, end as workflow ID
- `find_last_successful` only: Find start from last successful, end as
commit SHA
- `workflow_mode` only: Both start and end as workflow IDs
- Neither: Both start and end as commit SHAs

## Test Plan

- [x] Trigger workflow manually with
`find_last_successful=ci_nightly.yml` and hardcoded parameters
- [x] Verify report is generated and uploaded to S3
- [x] Verify step summary appears in GitHub Actions UI

workflow
mode)](https://github.com/ROCm/TheRock/actions/runs/23024165253/job/66867809624#step:3:2)
Test Case 2: [[CI] Manifest-diff: Test Case 2 (find-last-successful, end
as
SHA)](https://github.com/ROCm/TheRock/actions/runs/23024353822/job/66868457526#step:3:2)
Test Case 3: [[CI] Manifest-diff: Test Case 3 (workflow mode, start/end
as run
IDs)](https://github.com/ROCm/TheRock/actions/runs/23024506197/job/66868997210#step:3:2)
Test Case 4: [[CI] Manifest-diff: Test Case 4 (start/end as commit
SHAs)](https://github.com/ROCm/TheRock/actions/runs/23024684367/job/66869608549#step:3:2)

commit.message:
[#176] Replace lightbox with Spotlight.js for docs image lightbox (#330)

Implements #176.

The original lightbox2 library renders SVG images oversized. This PR
replaces it with
[Spotlight.js](https://github.com/nextapps-de/spotlight), a lightweight,
dependency-free modern lightbox library.

## Summary

- Replace `sphinxcontrib-lightbox2` with a new `sphinxcontrib-spotlight`
Sphinx extension
- The extension bundles Spotlight.js v0.7.8 (single 22KB bundle, vs 70KB
for glightbox)
- All documentation images are automatically wrapped in `<a
class="spotlight">` anchors via a Sphinx post-transform
- Images can be excluded per-image with the `no-spotlight` CSS class

## Changes

**New package** `py_packages/sphinxcontrib_spotlight/`:
- Bundles Spotlight.js v0.7.8 as a single self-contained JS bundle
(includes CSS)
- Custom `spotlight_reference` node defers URI resolution to write time,
ensuring hrefs point to `_images/` output paths rather than source paths
- `SpotlightTransform` post-transform wraps all `image` nodes
automatically
- Alt text becomes `data-description` for the lightbox caption
- Spotlight auto-initializes from markup — no manual JS init script
needed

**Updated configuration:**
- `docs/source/conf.py`: switched extension from
`sphinxcontrib.lightbox2` → `sphinxcontrib.spotlight`, removed
`lightbox2_*` config options
- `pyproject.toml`: updated dependency and uv editable source

## Testing

- [x] Sphinx build completes without errors
- [x] Extension loads correctly in the docs venv
(`sphinxcontrib.spotlight` importable)
- [x] Old `sphinxcontrib-glightbox` and `sphinxcontrib-lightbox2`
packages removed from venv

commit.message:
Show directory picker when creating customizations with multiple source folders (#301250)

* feat: auto-select single source folder in prompt selection

* Show directory picker when creating customizations with multiple source folders

When the Sessions app creates a new customization (agent, skill,
instructions, prompt), the target directory was always auto-resolved
to the first default location (e.g., .github/agents). This ignored
that the system may be reading from multiple directories (e.g.,
.github/agents and .claude/agents).

Now, if multiple source folders exist for the target storage type,
a quick pick is shown to let the user choose. If only one folder
exists, it is used directly without prompting.

This applies to both the manual creation flow (createNewItemManual)
and the AI-guided creation flow (createWithAI) in the management
editor.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: update accessibilitySupport and fontInfo types to any, and placeholder type to string | undefined

* Fix regression: pass undefined targetDir through to command

When no matching folder exists for the requested storage type (e.g.
skills have no user-storage folder), the previous code silently
aborted. The original behavior passed undefined through, letting
the command show its own folder picker via askForPromptSourceFolder.

Use null to signal user cancellation (dismiss picker) vs undefined
to signal no folder available. Also address review feedback:
- Use folder directly when length=1 in customizationCreatorService
- Use generic localized placeholder string
- Revert unrelated askForPromptSourceFolder.ts change

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Filter workspace picker to project root children only

getSourceFolders() tags tilde-expanded user paths (e.g. ~/.claude/agents)
as PromptsStorage.local, causing them to appear in the workspace picker.
Filter local folders to only those under the active project root so only
actual workspace directories are shown.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add 'New AGENTS.md' option to Instructions dropdown in Sessions

When in the Instructions section of the Sessions app, the + button
dropdown now includes a 'New AGENTS.md' option that creates the file
at the workspace root. Uses the AGENT_MD_FILENAME constant from
promptFileLocations.ts — no hardcoded filenames.

If the file already exists, it opens it in the embedded editor.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix: prevent unfiltered folder picker when no matching storage exists

When the user clicks 'New Skill (User)' but no user-storage folder
exists for skills, the fallback returned undefined which was passed
through to the command. The command's askForPromptSourceFolder then
showed ALL folders including workspace-scoped ones.

Now when both the storage filter and the legacy fallback return
nothing, we return null (cancellation) instead of undefined
(pass-through), preventing the unfiltered picker from appearing.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix: use project root as boundary instead of unreliable storage tags

The storage tags from getSourceFolders() are unreliable — tilde-expanded
user paths like ~/.copilot/skills get tagged PromptsStorage.local by
getConfigBasedSourceFolders(). This caused the user picker to show
workspace folders and vice versa.

Instead, partition folders by whether they're under the active project
root: children of project root are workspace, everything else is user.
This correctly handles all customization types including skills.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix duplicate user dirs: prefer ~/.copilot as creation target

getSourceFolders() returns both ~/.claude/agents and ~/.copilot/agents
for user targets. The Sessions app's canonical creation target is
~/.copilot (per AgenticPromptsService design). Use the workspace
service's first includedUserFileRoot (~/.copilot) to filter user
folders for creation, and deduplicate by URI to remove duplicates
from config-based and override sources.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Show all user targets, just deduplicate by URI

Don't filter out user directories — show everything we read from.
The only issue was duplicates (e.g. ~/.copilot/agents appearing twice
from config-based and override sources), which the URI dedup handles.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address review feedback: dedup, telemetry, empty folder handling

- customizationCreatorService: deduplicate workspace folders by URI
  before length checks to avoid unnecessary picker with duplicates
- managementEditor: normalize workspace-root to workspace in telemetry
  to avoid breaking the documented schema
- managementEditor: return undefined (not null) when no matching folders
  exist, so the command can fall back to askForPromptSourceFolder

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix: update accessibilitySupport and fontInfo types to any, and placeholder type to string | undefined

* Remove monaco.d.ts from branch diff

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Track head SHA to detect cloud agent work completion on merge conflicts (#600)

* Initial plan

* Track head SHA in PendingMergeIssue to detect agent work completion

When the cloud_agent backend posts a @copilot comment on a conflicting
PR, we now record the PR's head SHA at comment time. In
promote_pending_merge_issues, if the PR still has conflicts but the head
SHA changed (indicating the agent pushed a change), the deduplication
guard is removed so a fresh comment can be posted. This prevents the
system from staying silent indefinitely when the agent finishes work but
fails to resolve conflicts.

commit.message:
Forms: Create synced form when inserting form variations via block inserter (#47335)

* Forms: Create synced form when inserting form variations via block inserter

When users insert a form variation directly from the block inserter (e.g.,
"Contact Form"), WordPress creates the block with innerBlocks immediately,
bypassing the VariationPicker. This meant synced forms were not being created.

This adds a new hook `useCreateSyncedFormOnInsertion` that detects when a form
block is inserted with inner blocks but without a ref attribute, and
automatically creates a synced form when central form management is enabled.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Apply suggestions from code review

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Forms: Use variationName attribute and wasBlockJustInserted for synced form creation

Simplify the useCreateSyncedFormOnInsertion hook by:
- Using wasBlockJustInserted selector to detect newly inserted blocks
- Using variationName attribute directly instead of deriving variation from inner blocks
- Removing complex heuristic-based variation detection logic

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Forms: Address review feedback for synced form creation hook

- Add entity preloading before setting ref to prevent skeleton flash
- Replace unstable useEffect deps with empty array and ref guard
- Remove unnecessary registry.batch() wrapper around single dispatch
- Update getVariationTitle docblock to accurately describe return cases
- Replace flaky setTimeout in tests with deterministic microtask flushing

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Forms: Simplify synced form creation hook for performance

- Short-circuit useSelect after creation attempt to stop unnecessary store queries
- Collapse guard clauses into single shouldCreate boolean
- Match variations by name instead of attributes.variationName
- Add variationName to variations missing it so name-based lookup works
- Simplify tests by removing redundant mock data

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Forms: Fix TypeScript error for currentPostId type mismatch

getCurrentPostId() returns string | number, but createSyncedForm
expects number. Wrap with Number() to satisfy the type checker.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Use variationName in insertion logic

Only create a synced form on insertion if the block has a variationName: add a truthy attributes.variationName check to the shouldCreate condition and include it in the dependency array. Also fix the variation title lookup to compare against variations' attributes.variationName (not v.name) so the correct form title is selected.

* Prevent post-unmount updates in form insertion hook

Add cancellation handling and safer entity preload in useCreateSyncedFormOnInsertion: introduce a cancelled flag with a cleanup return to avoid setting attributes or showing notices after unmount, wrap getEntityRecord in try/catch as a best-effort preload, and bail early when cancelled. Update tests to cover preload failure (still sets ref), adjust variationName test data, ensure no synced form is created when variationName is missing, and add a test for unknown variationName fallback behavior.

* Forms: Add useEffect dependencies to fix synced form creation for variations

The empty dependency array meant the effect only ran on mount. If
shouldCreate was false initially (e.g. innerBlocks not yet populated),
the synced form would never be created. The hasAttemptedCreation ref
prevents double execution.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Reset creation flag on cleanup and silence deps

Reset hasAttemptedCreation.current to false in the effect cleanup so the creation attempt flag is cleared when the component is unmounted. The effect's dependency array was replaced with an empty array and an eslint-disable comment was added to suppress react-hooks/exhaustive-deps; this makes the effect run only once on mount (intentional to avoid repeated creation attempts). Previously the effect listed shouldCreate, attributes, innerBlocks, currentPostId, setAttributes, createSuccessNotice, and createErrorNotice as dependencies.

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: correctly extract LoginResponseDTO from API response wrapper in refresh token interceptor

The interceptor was casting response.data as LoginResponseDTO, so user/
accessToken/refreshToken all resolved to undefined → null, clearing
localStorage on every successful token refresh.

fields are read and localStorage is updated instead of cleared.

commit.message:
[build] E: Batch cargo invocations

Replace per-package cargo check/build/clippy/fmt invocations with
batched multi-package calls using repeated -p flags. Packages sharing
the same target triple and feature flags are grouped into a single
cargo invocation. This eliminates redundant workspace resolution,
dependency fingerprinting, and lock acquisition overhead.

Before: 62 separate cargo invocations for `make check`
After:  16 batched cargo invocations for `make check`

The batched -p flag lists are auto-generated from the same top-level
package lists (ALL_GUEST_RUST_LIBS, ALL_HOST_BINARIES, etc.) that
drove the previous foreach macros. Adding a new package still only
requires appending to the appropriate list.

Individual per-package targets (e.g., check-guest-rlib-nvx) are
preserved for ad-hoc developer use.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: multi-domain subdomain support (#8)

* Initial plan

* feat: add multi-domain subdomain support

- Config: add DOMAINS env var (JSON list of {domain, cloudflare_zone_id})
  with backward-compat fallback to BASE_DOMAIN/CLOUDFLARE_ZONE_ID
- Models: add domain column to Subdomain, unique constraint on (slug, domain)
- Schemas: add domain field to SubdomainPurchase, SubdomainResponse,
  SubdomainCheckResponse
- Routes: validate domain against config, use domain in all queries,
  add ?domain query param to set_origin, check, and delete endpoints
- Cloudflare: look up zone_id per domain
- Migration 008: add domain column, update unique constraint
- Router: support multiple domains via CONFIGURED_DOMAINS list
- Dashboard: domain selector in purchase form, full slug.domain in list
- Tests: update existing tests, add multi-domain integration tests

Co-authored-by: Bigalan09 <800520+Bigalan09@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: remove unnecessary String() conversion in dashboard template
